Bagging

Author

Thimoté Dupuch

Published

June 14, 2026

using CairoMakie, Statistics, Random, LinearAlgebra, SpecialFunctions
blue, orange, green, pink, teal, red, yellow = Makie.wong_colors()
function bagging_1nn(x, y, xtest; m, nrep)
    n = length(x)
    nloc = round(Int, n / m)
    ypred = zeros(length(xtest), nrep)

    for irep in 1:nrep
        idx = randperm(n)[1:nloc]
        xs, ys = x[idx], y[idx]

        dists = abs2.(xs .- xtest')
        nn_idx = [argmin(dists[:, j]) for j in axes(dists, 2)]
        ypred[:, irep] = ys[nn_idx]
    end
    return mean(ypred, dims=2) |> vec
end

xtest = 0:0.001:1
fxtest = sin.(2π .* xtest)

std_noise = 0.35
n = 200
x = collect(0:(n-1)) ./ n
y = sin.(2π .* x) .+ std_noise * randn(n)

ms = sort(unique(round.(Int, 2.0 .^(0:7))))
nrep = 100

fig = Figure(size=(1200, 600))

for (im, m) in enumerate(ms)
    ypred_mean = bagging_1nn(x, y, xtest; m=m, nrep=nrep)
    error = mean((ypred_mean .- fxtest).^2)

    ax = Axis(fig[cld(im, 4), mod1(im, 4)],
        title = "n/s = $m - error = $(round(error, digits=3))",
    )
    ylims!(ax, (-1.5, 1.6))

    lines!(ax, xtest, ypred_mean, color=blue, linewidth=2, label="bagging")
    lines!(ax, xtest, fxtest, color=red, linewidth=2, label="target")
    scatter!(ax, x, y, marker=:x, color=:black, markersize=8, label="data")

    if im == 1
        axislegend(ax, position=:rt)
    end
end

fig
